def _backoff(attempt: int, base: float = 0.25) -> float:
    # Exponential backoff with jitter; capped at 30s to avoid thundering herd.

The `--ignore-existing` flag tells rsync to skip updating files that already exist on the destination, regardless of whether the source copy is newer. The `--ignore-unmatched` flag tells `tar` to skip over files specified on the command line that don't exist in the archive, rather than failing with an error. Combine with `--ignore-failed-read` if you also want to suppress errors on unreadable source files during creation. Note that `--ignore-zeros` is unrelated — it instructs tar to keep reading past zero-filled blocks, which is useful for concatenated archives.

The `--ignore-existing` flag tells rsync to skip files that already exist on the destination, regardless of timestamp or size. Pair it with `--ignore-times` only if you specifically want to force a re-check of mtime-equal files; otherwise the two flags work at cross purposes. Note that `--ignore-errors` is unrelated and only affects how rsync handles I/O errors during the `--delete` phase.
